name: Zig Armory
description: The armory gleams with the cold blue light of comptime evaluation. Racks of weapons line the walls — each one a cross-compiled binary, sharp and ready for any target. The air is thin and precise, like the scent of fresh metal shavings. An anvil in the center is engraved with '@cImport', the incantation that lets Zig wear C's armor.
look_text: The armory is organized by compilation target. Left wall: ARM and RISC-V cross-compilation racks, their targets labeled in chalk. Center: the comptime forge, where code runs at compile time to generate optimized runtime code. Right wall: the C import station, where existing C libraries are wrapped in Zig safety without wrapper overhead. The floor is a grid of target triples.

exits.description: The forge, where ownership is hammered into shape
The bright studio, where types flow like water
The vast library, where ecosystems grow

recipes.name: cross-compiled snap
recipes.description: A snap binary cross-compiled from x86 to ARM, RISC-V, and WASM in a single zig build command. Zero dependencies, zero sysroot setup.

recipes.name: c-import snap wrapper
recipes.description: Zig wrapper around the C single-header snap library. @cImport generates type-safe bindings automatically. Zig safety with C performance.
